prompt: An onSubmit client script returns false. What happens?
choices: The server business rules are skipped permanently
explanation: Returning false from an onSubmit client script cancels the form submission, commonly used for client-side validation.

prompt: Which pair correctly contrasts UI Policies and Client Scripts?
choices: UI Policies are declarative for field state; Client Scripts run custom JavaScript on form events
UI Policies run only in email; Client Scripts run only on the server
Both can only run in scheduled jobs
Neither can affect the same field
explanation: UI Policies declaratively control field visibility/mandatory/read-only, while client scripts execute JavaScript on load, change, submit, or cell edit.

prompt: Which client script method displays a temporary informational message at the top of the form?
explanation: g_form.addInfoMessage() shows a client-side info message on the form; gs methods are server-side.

prompt: When a business rule needs to run without blocking the user after the record is saved, which execution timing is appropriate?
explanation: An async business rule runs in a scheduled job after the transaction, avoiding delays to the user while performing follow-up work.

prompt: Which methods are valid ways to make a field read-only from a client script? (Choose two.)
explanation: g_form.setReadOnly() and its alias g_form.setDisabled() both make a field read-only on the client; gs and current are server-side objects.
